headline: Draft's locked. 48 picks in. Two days until Mexico kicks the door open.
sub: Pre-tournament read: James drew the best roster on paper, Joe made the boldest bet of the night, and Nick has more elite tier-1 talent than anyone — at exactly the worst multiplier. Outlook below.

predictedStandings.name: James
predictedStandings.summary: Quietly assembled the best roster of the night. Norway at 3x means Haaland is a points printer if he gets going. Morocco's 2022 semifinal floor + Dutch ceiling = real outlook to lead wire-to-wire.

predictedStandings.name: Nick
predictedStandings.summary: Argentina AND Portugal is the strongest top-end on the board. Catch: both are 1x. Bigger catch: Austria sits in Group J right next to Argentina, so Nick's own teams cannibalize for points.

predictedStandings.name: Joe
predictedStandings.summary: Brazil is the trophy hold. The Group L stack (Croatia / Ghana / Panama, all opposite England) is either a tournament-winning concentration play or a Round-of-32 obituary. No middle ground.

predictedStandings.name: Rob
predictedStandings.summary: France-Mexico-Colombia is the most balanced top three in the league. Group I (France-Senegal-Iraq-Norway) is a meat grinder. If France escapes, Rob's ceiling is the final.

predictedStandings.name: Jim
predictedStandings.summary: England-centric. Uruguay and Ivory Coast are clean mid-tier hedges. Floor is high, but ceiling depends entirely on whether Bellingham delivers the run England has owed since 1966.

predictedStandings.name: Teddy
predictedStandings.summary: Spain at 1x is the rock. The real intrigue is Canada at 3x as a host — if Davies-Alphonso-David push past group stage, this roster moves. Tunisia/Haiti/Bosnia are lottery tickets.

predictedStandings.name: Stev
predictedStandings.summary: Germany anchors. Then it gets messy: Senegal AND Iraq both drew Group I (with France) so Stev is racing two of his own teams for a knockout slot. Japan/Turkey have real upside.

predictedStandings.name: Max
predictedStandings.summary: The safety play. Most spread-out roster (6 groups, 6 teams). Belgium has to be the engine. Otherwise it's a Tier-2 grind: Switzerland, Ecuador, South Korea — all live, none thrilling.

storylines.title: The Group L Gambit
storylines.body: Joe drafted Croatia, Ghana, AND Panama — all in Group L, all opposite Jim's England. The math: England takes one qualifying spot, leaving two for Joe's three teams. He's either lapping the field by July 1 or down 0-12 in fantasy points he literally drafted onto himself.

storylines.title: James's Haaland Hedge
storylines.body: Norway is Tier 3 (3x). Haaland is the best striker on Earth. Group I is brutal (France, Senegal, Iraq) but if Norway escapes and wins a knockout round, that's 6+ points per game versus 2-3 from elite Tier-1s. This is the highest-leverage pick of the draft.

storylines.title: Nick's Own-Goal Handcuff
storylines.body: Argentina and Austria both drew Group J. Nick's reigning-champion pick will produce points; his fourth-round pick will also produce points — but every Argentina-Austria result is a wash for him. Best case his teams finish 1st and 2nd. Worst case they trade losses with Algeria (Rob's).

storylines.title: Teddy's Host Card
storylines.body: Canada (T3, 3x) is the sleeper of the draft. Hosts have advanced from group stage in 4 of the last 5 World Cups. If Canada survives Group B and wins ONE knockout match, that's 9 points from a single pick — more than most Tier-1s will produce all tournament.

storylines.title: Saudi Arabia Beat Argentina Once
storylines.body: Stev's last pick was Saudi Arabia (T4, 4x). They share Group H with Spain. A 2022-style upset is worth 4 points for a single group win, and an R32 win is worth EIGHT. The lowest-tier teams have the highest variance — watch them.

groupNarratives.headline: Mexico's pressure cooker
groupNarratives.lead: Hosts open the tournament June 11. South Korea is the real threat to Mexico's qualifying floor.

groupNarratives.headline: Wide open
groupNarratives.lead: No clear favorite. Canada will be backed by 30,000 in Toronto. Switzerland is the steady pick. Qatar and Bosnia are wild cards.

groupNarratives.headline: Brazil's redemption tour
groupNarratives.lead: Joe's Brazil pick should walk to first. Morocco is the dangerous floor for Joe — and the dangerous ceiling for James.

groupNarratives.headline: USA's home stage
groupNarratives.lead: Joe's USA pick gets the home crowd. Paraguay is the worry. Turkey has top-shelf talent in Güler.

groupNarratives.headline: Germany's road test
groupNarratives.lead: Stev's Germany should top this. Ecuador and Ivory Coast are sneaky strong third-place candidates.

groupNarratives.headline: Netherlands' soft landing
groupNarratives.lead: James's Dutch pick gets a friendly group. Japan beat Germany AND Spain in 2022. Watch the Japan-Netherlands match.

groupNarratives.headline: Belgium's last dance
groupNarratives.lead: De Bruyne-Lukaku finale. Iran defends, Egypt has Salah, New Zealand is along for the ride.

groupNarratives.headline: Spain's coronation lap
groupNarratives.lead: Teddy's Spain pick is the cleanest read in the draft. Uruguay and Saudi Arabia fight for second.
groupNarratives.ownerSpread: Teddy (Spain), Jim (Uruguay), Stev (Saudi Arabia), Rob (Cape Verde)

groupNarratives.headline: The Group of Death
groupNarratives.lead: France vs Norway vs Senegal in one group. Iraq just there to absorb damage. Two of these advance — picking which is impossible.

groupNarratives.headline: Argentina's victory lap
groupNarratives.lead: Reigning champs. Austria-Algeria fight for second. Jordan completes the field. Nick has two teams here.

groupNarratives.headline: Portugal-Colombia coin flip
groupNarratives.lead: Two Tier-1s in the same group means one of Nick/Rob's stars eats a tough draw. DR Congo could shock somebody.

groupNarratives.headline: Joe's stack vs Jim's England
groupNarratives.lead: England should win this. Croatia is dangerous in knockouts. Joe owns three of four teams here. Storyline of the group stage.

teamBlurbs.France: Mbappé in his prime, depth at every position, 2018 winners and 2022 finalists. The most complete squad on paper. Group I is the only obstacle.
teamBlurbs.Spain: UEFA Champions and oddsmaker favorites. Yamal is the youngest superstar in the tournament. Group H is a soft draw.
teamBlurbs.Argentina: World #1. Reigning champs. Messi at 38 is one tournament tour away from the truest GOAT closer in sports.
teamBlurbs.England: Bellingham, Saka, Foden, Kane. Talent for days. Pressure forever. Group L is winnable; the knockout half is brutal.
teamBlurbs.Portugal: Ronaldo era over, Bruno-Félix-Neves era now. Reigning Nations League holders. Group K with Colombia is the real test.
teamBlurbs.Brazil: Ancelotti's first World Cup as Brazil manager. Vinicius, Rodrygo, Raphinha. National mood: furious about the wait since 2002.
teamBlurbs.Netherlands: Underrated, like always. Frenkie de Jong directs traffic, Gakpo finishes. Group F is the softest landing of any Tier-1.
teamBlurbs.Germany: Nagelsmann's project. Wirtz is the X-factor. Hosted last Euro, now a road game across the Atlantic.
teamBlurbs.Morocco: 2022 semifinalists. Hakimi, Ziyech, En-Nesyri. Not a Cinderella anymore — a known quantity. Joe and James are watching closely.
teamBlurbs.Belgium: Last ride for De Bruyne and Lukaku. Tijjani Reijnders too late to fix everything. Real Tier-1 ceiling though.
teamBlurbs.Croatia: Modrić, 40 years old, still refuses to leave the field. Always plays into the back half of the bracket.
teamBlurbs.Colombia: Copa America runners-up. James Rodríguez 2.0 era. Group K has two Tier-1s; only one advances comfortably.
teamBlurbs.Senegal: Reigning AFCON champions. Sadio Mané evergreen. Drew the worst possible group (France, Norway, Iraq).
teamBlurbs.Mexico: Hosts. Pressure city. Opening match June 11. Always overperforms in groups, always underperforms in R16.
teamBlurbs.United States: Pulisic-Reyna-Balogun core in their prime. Home crowd in three host cities. Realistically a QF ceiling — but in this format that's elite.
teamBlurbs.Uruguay: Bielsa's chaos. Always overperforms the talent. Núñez/Pellistri/Valverde core. Group H second place is theirs to lose.
teamBlurbs.Japan: Beat Germany AND Spain in 2022. Mitoma in form. Group F could be Japan's path to a QF for the first time ever.
teamBlurbs.Switzerland: Yakin's pragmatists. Group B is wide open; Switzerland is the steadiest team in it.
teamBlurbs.Iran: Group G underdog. Defense first, counter when it opens. Always a tough out.
teamBlurbs.Turkey: Montella's energy plus Arda Güler's wand. Stev's sleeper pick has real upside.
teamBlurbs.Ecuador: Always shows up. Solid defense, Caicedo runs the middle.
teamBlurbs.Austria: Rangnick's pressing system. Real threat in Group J — Nick's other pick here might out-earn Argentina in group play.
teamBlurbs.South Korea: Son Heung-min's last World Cup. Group A is genuinely winnable.
teamBlurbs.Australia: Always punches up. Out of group in 2022. James's clever late grab.
teamBlurbs.Algeria: Mahrez's swan song. Group J is a tough lane.
teamBlurbs.Egypt: Salah's last shot at a World Cup moment. Group G has openings.
teamBlurbs.Canada: Host nation. Davies-Alphonso-David is a real attack. Teddy's 3x multiplier sleeper.
teamBlurbs.Norway: Haaland. That's the sentence. 3x multiplier on the best striker alive — highest-leverage pick on the board.
teamBlurbs.Panama: Defensive shell, set-piece grinders. Group L survivor candidate.
teamBlurbs.Ivory Coast: Reigning AFCON champions. Probably the strongest African team after Morocco. Jim got real value here.
teamBlurbs.Sweden: Isak and Gyökeres up top, a question mark behind them. Group F third place is on the table.
teamBlurbs.Paraguay: Set-piece grinders. Defense first. Solid 4x lottery ticket if they sneak past USA or Australia.
teamBlurbs.Czechia: Old-guard meets young-guard. Group A is live; Czechia could finish second.
teamBlurbs.Scotland: Brave little Scotland. The Tartan Army will be everywhere. Group C is a brutal draw though.
teamBlurbs.Tunisia: Defensive shape, always a tough out, rarely scores. Floor is high, ceiling is low.
teamBlurbs.DR Congo: AFCON semifinalists. Biggest African dark horse. Group K could get interesting.
teamBlurbs.Uzbekistan: First-ever World Cup. Vibes are pure. Realistically here to absorb damage in Group K.
teamBlurbs.Qatar: Defending hosts in someone else's tournament. The drop-off is steep.
teamBlurbs.Iraq: Group I dragon's lair. Worst possible draw for a Tier-4 team. Stev gambled.
teamBlurbs.South Africa: Host group A guest. AFCON semifinalist 2024. Don't sleep.
teamBlurbs.Saudi Arabia: Beat Argentina in 2022. The single most dangerous Tier-4 team. Stev's last pick could be brilliant.
teamBlurbs.Jordan: Asian Cup finalists 2024. Group J just hopes to take a point off someone.
teamBlurbs.Bosnia and Herzegovina: Džeko's legacy run, age 40. Group B is open enough that Bosnia could steal third place.
teamBlurbs.Cape Verde: First ever World Cup. The whole country bought tickets. Group H is brutal but the vibes are immaculate.
teamBlurbs.Ghana: Black Stars looking to atone for the 2022 fiasco. Group L means battling Joe's other two teams.
teamBlurbs.Curacao: Population: 150,000. World Cup participants. Vibes: immaculate. Group E is rough.
teamBlurbs.Haiti: First World Cup since 1974. Earthquake nation gets a global stage. Group C is a brutal welcome back.
teamBlurbs.New Zealand: All Whites back in the WC after long absence. Group G is winnable for third place.
